# Designing Event-Driven Systems (Ben Stopford)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [The Origins of Streaming](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/01-the-origins-of-streaming.md): Designing, implementing, or reviewing real-time data ingestion pipelines, stream processing architectures, Kafka Streams applications, KSQL scripts, or transitioning traditional batch/RPC-based analytics into continuous, event-driven streaming systems.
    - [Is Kafka What You Think It Is?](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/02-is-kafka-what-you-think-it-is.md): Designing, refactoring, or reviewing architectures involving Apache Kafka, specifically concerning microservices communication protocols, legacy system integration, data pipelines, and stream processing topologies.
    - [Beyond Messaging: An Overview of the Kafka Broker](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/03-beyond-messaging-an-overview-of-the-kafka-broker.md): Designing, configuring, or implementing Apache Kafka broker infrastructure, including topic configurations, cluster scaling, producer/consumer properties for durability and ordering, long-term storage or compacted topics, security, tenant segregation, and quotas.
    - [Events: A Basis for Collaboration](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/04-events-a-basis-for-collaboration.md): When designing, analyzing, or implementing inter-service communication, distributed systems, event-driven architectures, microservice integrations, or cross-team data sharing mechanisms.
    - [Processing Events with Stateful Functions](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/05-processing-events-with-stateful-functions.md): Designing, analyzing, refactoring, or implementing distributed systems, microservices, event-driven architectures, or data pipelines, specifically those involving message brokers (e.g., Apache Kafka), stream processing frameworks (e.g., Kafka Streams, KSQL), data enrichments, or asynchronous service integrations.
    - [Event Sourcing, CQRS, and Other Stateful Patterns](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/06-event-sourcing-cqrs-and-other-stateful-patterns.md): Designing, reviewing, or implementing event-driven architectures, Kafka-based stream processing, stateful services, distributed data synchronization, data persistence strategies, or migrating legacy CRUD databases to modern event-centric models.
    - [Sharing Data and Services Across an Organization](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/07-sharing-data-and-services-across-an-organization.md): Distributed system architecture, microservices design, inter-team data sharing, API design, event-driven system refactoring, or resolving data integration and coupling issues across multiple organizational boundaries.
    - [Event Streams as a Shared Source of Truth](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/08-event-streams-as-a-shared-source-of-truth.md): Architectural design, system architecture planning, stream processing implementation, data integration strategies across distributed teams or services, designing Kafka-based multiteam systems, building materialized views, or implementing stateful stream processing.
    - [Lean Data](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/09-lean-data.md): Triggered when the AI is tasked with designing, implementing, or refactoring data storage layers, streaming pipelines, database views, ETL processes, or microservice data integrations within an event-driven or Apache Kafka-based architecture.
    - [Consistency and Concurrency in Event-Driven Systems](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/10-consistency-and-concurrency-in-event-driven-systems.md): Designing, refactoring, or writing code for event-driven systems, microservices architectures, Apache Kafka integrations, topic modeling, state mutation logic, distributed data consistency, or handling concurrency in asynchronous environments.
    - [Transactions, but Not as We Know Them](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/11-transactions-but-not-as-we-know-them.md): Designing, implementing, or debugging event-driven microservices, Kafka producers and consumers, stateful stream processing, or data consistency mechanisms (specifically deduplication, atomic multi-topic writes, exactly-once processing, and managing distributed application state) using Apache Kafka or Kafka Streams.
    - [Evolving Schemas and Data over Time](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/12-evolving-schemas-and-data-over-time.md): Designing, modifying, or reviewing event-driven data schemas, Kafka topics, payload structures, schema migrations, message error-handling mechanisms, or data deletion/GDPR compliance strategies in a streaming architecture.
    - [Kafka Streams and KSQL](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/13-kafka-streams-and-ksql.md): Designing, implementing, or debugging event-driven microservices, real-time analytics, or data processing pipelines using Apache Kafka, Kafka Streams API, or KSQL, including architectural planning, stream-topology definition, and writing code for stateful or stateless stream processors.
    - [Building Streaming Services](.roo/vendor/architecture/designing-event-driven-systems-ben-stopford/14-building-streaming-services.md): Designing, architecting, implementing, or refactoring streaming services, stateful stream processing applications, or event-driven microservices using Apache Kafka, Kafka Streams, or KSQL, including handling data topologies, event-sourced views, CQRS implementations, stream joins, and concurrent stateful operations.
